headlines.title: Chris Mason: Why Starmer thinks he's called it right on war despite Trump barbs
headlines.description: The BBC's Political Editor Chris Mason considers the US president's recent jabs at the UK prime minister.

headlines.title: Will Scotland be the first nation in the UK to legalise assisted dying?
headlines.description: The final vote on the Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults (Scotland) Bill is seen as too close to call.

headlines.title: Reeves vows to stop UK tech from 'drifting abroad'
headlines.description: The Chancellor tells the BBC she wants the "pattern to end" while also pledging closer ties with the EU

headlines.title: Dozens killed after Afghanistan rehab centre struck
headlines.description: The BBC visited the facility and saw more than 30 bodies being carried out on stretchers.

headlines.title: Easter holidaymakers switching from Dubai to Spain as flights fill up
headlines.description: It comes after the war in Iran caused mass disruption to flights across the Middle East and UAE.

headlines.title: Train Delay Repay rule changes to make claims easier
headlines.description: There will also be additional checks on railcards during a trial to crack down on fraud.

headlines.title: Moving English tests for migrants online risks criminal abuse, providers warn
headlines.description: Moving visa tests online could open the door to fraudsters and criminal gangs, a letter to government warns.

headlines.title: Spring to make a comeback with warmest day of the year forecast
headlines.description: Temperatures are on the rise again this week and as hours of daylight overtake hours of darkness, it will feel like spring has returned.

headlines.title: Millions without electricity as Cuba's power grid collapses
headlines.description: Cuba's chronic fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US blockade on oil shipments to the island.

headlines.title: And the burger goes to... Michael B Jordan marks Oscars win at fast food chain
headlines.description: Hours after winning best actor, Jordan joins an exclusive list of famous faces to celebrate an Oscar victory with a burger.

headlines.title: Trump's confidence is undimmed - but every Iran option comes with risk
headlines.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

headlines.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
headlines.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
headlines.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

headlines.title: Trump 'not happy' with UK response to Iran conflict
headlines.description: It comes after Sir Keir Starmer said the UK was working with allies on a plan to protect the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: What are the symptoms of meningitis and is there a vaccine?
headlines.description: Two people have died following an outbreak of meningitis, including one student at the University of Kent.

headlines.title: 'My mother cried out one last time': Palestinian boy, 12, describes how Israeli forces killed his family in car
headlines.description: Khaled Bani Odeh's parents and two brothers were shot dead as they drove home from a shopping trip in the occupied West Bank.

headlines.title: Young Venezuelans voice hope and frustration as post-Maduro future unfolds
headlines.description: Young people who have only known one political system are adjusting to change after US forces seized Maduro.

headlines.title: India's outsourcing industry is worth $300bn. Can it survive AI?
headlines.description: Indian IT stocks have plunged as fears grow of AI disrupting back offices. But some say these are overblown.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Race to stop meningitis spreading' and 'Donald's Trumped'
headlines.description: The response to confirmed cases of meningitis in the UK and the US-Israeli conflict with Iran lead Tuesday's papers.

headlines.title: How Chelsea signed a star team with hidden payments
headlines.description: Chelsea have been hit with a Premier League record fine of £10.75m and handed a suspended transfer ban. Here's what the written reasons said.

headlines.title: Watch: Reunion resident gets close to lava from erupting volcano
headlines.description: Lava from the Piton de la Fournaise volcano reached the Indian ocean for the first time in 19 years.

headlines.title: Utah bereavement author found guilty of fatally poisoning her husband
headlines.description: A jury found that Kouri Richins killed her husband in March 2022 by poisoning him with a fentanyl-laced drink.

headlines.title: AI firm Anthropic seeks weapons expert to stop users from 'misuse'
headlines.description: The artificial intelligence firm says it wants to prevent "catastrophic misuse" of its systems.

headlines.title: Reform pledges to scrap VAT and green levies on energy bills
headlines.description: There are fears the war in Iran could lead to a surge in household energy bills.

headlines.title: 'Fire came from the sky and burned them' - life on the brink of civil war in South Sudan
headlines.description: Some 50,000 people are told to flee their homes as the army warns of a huge military offensive in Jonglei state.

headlines.title: Woman not shortlisted for job as 'car is too old'
headlines.description: Alanah Thomspon French says her application was not progressed as her car was more than 10 years old.

headlines.title: £18bn needed to fix England and Wales potholes, road surfacing experts estimate
headlines.description: Just 51% of the local road network in England and Wales is reported to be in good condition, research suggests.

headlines.title: Gerry Adams to testify in court over claims he had role in IRA bombings
headlines.description: Three men are seeking a ruling that Adams is personally liable for injuries they received in explosions in London and Manchester.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Can Starmer Keep Saying No To Trump?
headlines.description: Trump "not happy" with the UK as Starmer says it won't be drawn into wider Iran war.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5Live! Will Trump lose his MAGA base over Iran?
headlines.description: And should the US have been better prepared over the Strait of Hormuz?

headlines.title: How Chelsea signed a star team with hidden payments
headlines.description: Chelsea have been hit with a Premier League record fine of £10.75m and handed a suspended transfer ban. Here's what the written reasons said.

headlines.title: Inside NSB - the state school at the top of English rugby union
headlines.description: In a sport often dominated by private schools, Northampton School for Boys are one win away from securing a league and cup double.

headlines.title: 'Looking like the Hamilton of old' and potential rule changes - F1 Q&A
headlines.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your questions following the Chinese Grand Prix.

headlines.title: Arsenal trial and self-doubt: Valverde's journey to Real legend
headlines.description: Rejected by Arsenal as a teenager, Federico Valverde has transformed himself into a Real Madrid legend - and was the main man in their Champions League win over Manchester City last week.

headlines.title: Raducanu withdraws from Miami Open because of illness
headlines.description: Emma Raducanu withdraws from this week's Miami Open as she continues to recover from a recent illness.

headlines.title: Gavaskar claims Hundred signing 'contributing to Indian casualties'
headlines.description: Former India captain Sunil Gavaskar says Sunrisers Leeds’ signing of Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed “indirectly contributes to the deaths of Indian soldiers and civilians”.
